recent_decisions.title: Approval of Cabinet minutes from 21st January 2026
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Cabinet approved the minutes from the previous meeting without discussion. No additional costs were noted for local elections.
key_data_points: Minutes approved as a correct record
No additional costs for local elections

recent_decisions.title: £50m Levelling Up Funding for Safer, Greener Streets and Public Transport
recent_decisions.political_summary: £50m Levelling Up Funding will support projects in Woodnook and Countess Street, Accrington, and improve bus services (9, 464, M1, X41).
recent_decisions.financial_context: Levelling Up Funding for infrastructure projects
key_data_points: £50m allocated for Safer, Greener Streets and Public Transport
Projects in Woodnook and Countess Street
Bus service improvements for routes 9, 464, M1, X41

recent_decisions.title: £8m Health Centre Renovations in East Lancashire
recent_decisions.political_summary: £8m will refurbish three health centres, including Acorn Primary Health Care Centre, creating a 'Health Hub' with X-ray rooms and a Minor Injuries Unit.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Department of Health and Social Care funding
key_data_points: Acorn Health Hub to include X-ray rooms and Minor Injuries Unit

recent_decisions.title: Roofing works at Oswaldtwistle Civic Theatre
recent_decisions.political_summary: Ross Lee Construction will undertake roofing works for 27 weeks, starting 27th February 2026. Community use of the ground floor is planned during works.
key_data_points: Ground floor to be used for community purposes during works
Grant funding secured from Theatres in Trust

recent_decisions.title: Change of use from pub to adult day centre approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the change of use from The Plough Pub to an adult day centre, despite objections, with conditions to ensure accessibility. Vote: Approved (details not recorded).
key_data_points: 1 letter of objection, 1 letter of support
Operating times: 8am to 6pm, 7 days a week

motions.text: The application was approved with authority delegated to the Chief Planning and Transportation Officer to draft an additional condition to those listed in the report, including restrictions of the ramp access to the property.

recent_decisions.title: Extension and garage conversion refused due to impact on neighbors
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee refused a 2.3m extension and garage conversion at 58 Station Road, Rishton, due to objections from residents and concerns over loss of light and privacy. Vote: Refused (details not recorded).
key_data_points: No objections from Sports England or Environmental Health

motions.text: The application was refused as per the officer’s recommendations.

recent_decisions.title: Care home for 1 child refused despite officer recommendation
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee refused a care home application for 1 child at 4 James Avenue, Great Harwood, citing conflict with policy and housing supply concerns, despite officer recommendation for approval. Vote: Refused (details not recorded).
key_data_points: 1 objection from resident, 1 from Children’s Services
Planning Inspectorate had granted similar appeals

motions.text: Members voted to go against the officer decision and refuse the application on the following grounds: Conflict with Policy 1 of the SPG and removing a market dwelling at a time when the council cannot demonstrate a 5-year supply of housing.

recent_decisions.committee: Communities and Wellbeing Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Approval of meeting minutes from October 2025
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes from the previous meeting held on 13th October 2025.

recent_decisions.committee: Communities and Wellbeing Overview and Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Update on Disabled Facilities Grants (DFGs) and funding
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee received an update on Disabled Facilities Grants, including a £1.72m budget for 2025/26 and changes to grant policies, such as increasing the upper limit for some grants to £10,000.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Total funding for Disabled Facilities Grants in 2025/26, including carried forward funds.
key_data_points: Waiting list due to staff capacity limitations

recent_decisions.title: Food waste collection service to launch April 2026
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved a new weekly food waste collection service starting April 1, 2026, with pilot testing in Rishton and Oswaldtwistle. Households will receive caddies and liners.

recent_decisions.title: Freedom of the Borough awards for Haworth and Parkins
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council decided to award the Freedom of the Borough to Councillors Marlene Haworth and Dave Parkins for their long service, to be confirmed March 26, 2026.

recent_decisions.title: Fair Funding Review 2.0 settlement accepted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council accepted the improved Fair Funding Review 2.0 settlement after lobbying, with better financial position than initially expected. Budget details to follow.

recent_decisions.title: Article 4 Direction expanded to whole borough
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved expanding Article 4 Direction to control Houses in Multiple Occupation across the entire borough, with a report expected soon.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Minutes from Last Meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes from the previous meeting as a correct record. No vote recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of £0.681m Capital Programme Addition
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved an in-year addition of £0.681m to the capital programme for new projects. No vote recorded.
recent_decisions.financial_context: In-year addition to capital programme for new projects
key_data_points: Projects include Levelling Up Funding, Leisure Centres, Huncoat Garden Village

recent_decisions.title: Note Financial Position and Pressures
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the financial position and pressures facing the Council as of September 2025, including recruitment and Housing Benefit challenges.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Financial pressures and risks as of September 2025

recent_decisions.title: Note Treasury Management Activities
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the treasury management activities and performance against the approved strategy for Q2 2025/26.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of previous Cabinet meeting minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Cabinet unanimously approved the minutes from previous meetings as correct records.
key_data_points: Minutes from November and December 2025 meetings approved

motions.text: That the Minutes be received and approved as correct records.

recent_decisions.title: £50m Levelling Up Funding for Hyndburn projects
recent_decisions.political_summary: £50m Levelling Up Funding will support Safer, Greener and Healthier Streets and public transport improvements in Hyndburn. No vote was taken.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Levelling Up Funding for local projects
key_data_points: Projects in Woodnook and Countess Street

recent_decisions.title: £8m health centre renovations in East Lancashire
recent_decisions.political_summary: £8m will refurbish three health centres, including creating a Health Hub in Accrington with new X-Ray rooms and a Minor Injuries Unit. No vote was taken.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Department of Health and Social Care funding
key_data_points: Health Hub to include X-Ray rooms and Minor Injuries Unit
Project to be completed in 20 weeks

recent_decisions.title: £27-week roofing works at Oswaldtwistle Civic Theatre
recent_decisions.political_summary: Ross Lee Construction will undertake 27-week roofing works at Oswaldtwistle Civic Theatre starting 27th February 2026. No vote was taken.
key_data_points: Council exploring community use during works
Grant funding secured from Theatres in Trust

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee approves minutes of last meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Audit Committee unanimously approved the minutes of their previous meeting held on 22nd September 2025.

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee notes risk management report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the risk management report, including updates to the strategic, operational, and generic risk registers, with 4 high risks identified.
key_data_points: Risk 1027 upgraded from medium to high

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee notes audit plan progress
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the audit plan progress, with 96.33% of the plan completed for 2025/2026, and no issues reported in key audits.
key_data_points: Target: 98% by end of financial year
No issues reported in procurement, risk management, creditors, or household support fund audits

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee notes follow-up actions
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted follow-up actions on previous audits, with some actions partially or fully implemented and others outstanding.
key_data_points: 3 areas reviewed: Homelessness, Environmental Protection, Rent Reviews
Environmental Protection addendum report due March 2026

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee notes Economic Crime Act report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the report on the Economic Crime & Corporate Transparency Act 2023, with low risk assessed but policy and training required.
key_data_points: Refresher training on fraud prevention planned

recent_decisions.title: Audit Committee notes external audit update
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the external audit update, with a disclaimed opinion expected due to insufficient time for completion.
key_data_points: Disclaimed opinion expected for 2024/25 financial statements
No significant weaknesses identified in value for money arrangements

recent_decisions.title: Appointment of Chair for Planning Committee
recent_decisions.political_summary: Councillor Noordad Aziz was voted in as Chair by majority vote.

motions.text: Councillor Noordad Aziz was nominated and voted in by majority vote to Chair the 17th of December 2025 Planning Committee Meeting.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Previous Meeting Minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: Minutes from the November 2025 meeting were approved as a correct record.

motions.text: The Minutes of the last Planning Committee held on the 12th of November 2025 were submitted for approval as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Enforcement Update Noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: Committee noted 455 live enforcement cases and 154 new complaints since April 2025.

motions.text: The information was noted by the Committee.

recent_decisions.title: Enforcement Action Against Unauthorised Development
recent_decisions.political_summary: Committee approved confidential enforcement action against unauthorised development.

motions.text: To approve the resolution as set out in the confidential schedule to these minutes.
